name: Mecom v. Fitzsimmons
summary: The Supreme Court reversed a lower court's fraudulent joinder ruling, demonstrating that a dismissed party retains its status in the litigation pending appellate review.
significance: Respondent cited it to show that a party found to be fraudulently joined remains in the case subject to appellate review — the Court reversed the fraudulent joinder ruling, necessarily meaning the party had remained in the case throughout.
